## SpaceX Shares Close First Month Below Debut Price Despite Nasdaq-100 Index Entry

Sub-debut performance through Nasdaq-100 inclusion signals either supply overhang from early holders or insufficient institutional demand at current valuations — PM should watch whether the stock finds a floor as index mechanical flows clear, or whether the drift continues, which would challenge the post-IPO narrative.

- SPCX ended its first full month of trading below its debut price
- Underperformance persisted even through and after Nasdaq-100 inclusion — suggesting passive inflows did not offset selling pressure
- Combination of index entry and sub-debut pricing is a weak technical signal for near-term momentum
- Single-outlet sourcing; no specific price levels or volume data cited in the headline
